content.text:
I have enough context. Let me now present my understanding and the key architectural questions before drafting the plan.

---

## Findings Summary

**The current state across the 7 mentioned files (verified):**

| File | Helper | Behavior in **dev** | Behavior in **preview** | Behavior in **prod** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `apps/app/next.config.ts:25` (inline `platformUrl`) | `withProject` | `http://localhost:4112` | `https://lightfast-platform.vercel.app` ⚠️ | `https://lightfast-platform.vercel.app` |
| `apps/app/src/lib/project-urls.ts` (`platformUrl`) | `withProject` | same | same ⚠️ | same |
| `apps/app/src/lib/microfrontends.ts` (`wwwUrl`) | `resolveProjectUrl` | portless URL | `microfrontends.json` fallback `https://lightfast.ai` ⚠️ | same |
| `apps/www/src/lib/project-urls.ts` (`appUrl`) | `resolveProjectUrl` | portless URL | `https://lightfast.ai` ⚠️ | same |
| `apps/platform/src/lib/project-urls.ts` (`appUrl`) | `withProject` | `http://localhost:3024` | `https://lightfast.ai` ⚠️ (BUG — hits prod from preview) | `https://lightfast.ai` |
| `api/platform/src/lib/project-urls.ts` (`appUrl`) | `withProject` | same | same ⚠️ | same |

**Why every preview row is broken:** `VERCEL_RELATED_PROJECTS` is only injected when `vercel.json` declares `relatedProjects`. **None of the three `apps/*/vercel.json` files declare it** — they only have `ignoreCommand`. So `withProject` always returns `defaultHost`, and `resolveProjectUrl`'s non-dev branch always returns the `microfrontends.json` `development.fallback` (literally `"https://lightfast.ai"` for both apps).

Additionally, `resolveProjectUrl` **does not consult `VERCEL_RELATED_PROJECTS` at all** — only `withProject` does. The two helpers solve different problems and are being mixed.

**Key downstream blast radius (consumers of the URLs above):**
- `apps/app/.../api/trpc/[trpc]/route.ts:13` — CORS allowlist (uses `wwwUrl`).
- `apps/platform/.../api/trpc/[trpc]/route.ts:11` — strict `origin !== appUrl` CORS check.
- `apps/app/next.config.ts:103,107` — rewrites `/api/connect/*`, `/api/ingest/*` → `${platformUrl}`.
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(content)/docs/.../layout.tsx` — `signInUrl = ${appUrl}/sign-in` (×2).
- `api/platform/src/lib/provider-configs.ts:24` — OAuth callback base URL.

**Tangentially related code that bypasses the helpers entirely (also using the wrong env vars):**
- `api/platform/src/lib/oauth/authorize.ts:20-33` and `oauth/callback.ts:23-36` — inline `VERCEL_ENV`/`VERCEL_URL` reads, no VRP awareness.

**Web research confirmed:**
1. VRP is configured via `vercel.json` `relatedProjects: ["prj_..."]` (in code, not dashboard). Max 3 per app, same-repo only.
2. Vercel auto-injects `VERCEL_RELATED_PROJECTS` on every deployment of a project that declares related ones — including previews on any branch.
3. `preview.branch` is populated only when the related project has a deploy from the same branch. Otherwise the field is absent and helpers fall through to `defaultHost`.
4. **Microfrontends ≠ Related Projects.** They're separate Vercel features — `microfrontends.json` is network routing; VRP is env-var injection. Configuring one doesn't configure the other.
5. `@vercel/related-projects.withRelatedProject` and `@lightfastai/dev-proxy.withProject` have identical resolution logic.

**Desktop is a non-concern** — `apps/desktop/` only knows `appOrigin` (set by `with-desktop-env.mjs` via `resolvePortlessMfeUrl` in dev, hardcoded `https://lightfast.ai` in prod). Zero references to platform.

---

## Open Architectural Questions

Before I draft the plan, I need your judgment on three decisions:

**Q1 — Consolidation shape.** The 5 project-urls files plus the inline `next.config.ts` definition all do variants of the same lookup. Three options:

- **(A) Centralize in `vendor/url-resolver`** (or `@vendor/dev-proxy-helpers`) — a single helper `resolveProjectUrl({ projectName, env, fallback })` that handles dev (portless) + preview (VRP, throw on miss) + prod (hardcoded fallback). All 5 files become 2-line re-exports. Pro: one source of truth. Con: another package.
- **(B) Keep per-app `lib/project-urls.ts` files but make them all call a single new helper from `@vendor/dev-proxy-helpers`.** Same as (A) but the per-app file still owns the production fallback constant. Pro: per-app fallback stays close to the app. Con: still 5 small files.
- **(C) No new package — just standardize the implementation in each file** to call `withProject` + throw-on-preview-miss directly. Pro: zero new abstraction. Con: 5 copies of the same throw logic.

Recommendation: **(B)** — least invasive but eliminates the throw-logic duplication and the `withProject` vs `resolveProjectUrl` mix-up.

**Q2 — VRP-miss handling on preview.**

When `apps/app` deploys from `feature/foo` and `apps/platform` doesn't have a `feature/foo` deploy (turbo-ignore skipped it), `VERCEL_RELATED_PROJECTS.preview.branch` is absent for platform. Three options:

- **Fall back to `lightfast-platform-git-main-...vercel.app`** (the main-branch alias). Closer to "shared staging" semantics. Requires hard-coding the alias pattern.

Recommendation: **Throw**, plus update `turbo-ignore` (or each `vercel.json` `ignoreCommand`) to never skip a related sibling. The current "fall back silently to prod" is the bug we're fixing.

## Proposed Plan Structure

**Title**: `2026-05-05-pr630-related-projects-allowlist.md`

### Architecture

**New shared package**: `packages/related-projects/` (`@repo/related-projects`)

Single export:
```ts
createRelatedProjectResolver({
  projectName: string,         // matches the Vercel project name (e.g. "lightfast-app")
  productionFallback: string,  // hardcoded prod URL
  devFallback: string,         // local dev URL (e.g. "http://localhost:3024")
}): string
```

Logic:
- `VERCEL_ENV === "production"` → use `withProject` (or fall back to `productionFallback`)
- `VERCEL_ENV === "preview"` → use `withProject` and **throw** if VRP missing or sibling not in `relatedProjects` array
- Otherwise (dev/local) → return `devFallback`

Notes:
- Single helper replaces the current `withProject` vs `resolveProjectUrl` mix-up. Microfrontends portless dev URLs are still handled separately in `next.config.ts` (via `getPortlessMfeDevOrigins` + `withPortlessMfeDev`); they're a different problem.
- `devFallback` stays per-app (retains your option-B preference: per-app files own their fallbacks).

### Phases

**Phase 1 — Build `@repo/related-projects`**
- Scaffold package, dependency on `@lightfastai/dev-proxy`.
- Implement `createRelatedProjectResolver`.
- Unit tests: prod-VRP-hit, prod-VRP-miss-uses-fallback, preview-VRP-hit, preview-VRP-miss-throws, preview-project-not-in-VRP-throws, dev-returns-devFallback.

**Phase 2 — Declare `relatedProjects` in `vercel.json`**
- `apps/app/vercel.json` → `relatedProjects: ["<platform-id>", "<www-id>"]` (max 3, we use 2).
- `apps/platform/vercel.json` → `relatedProjects: ["<app-id>"]`.
- `apps/www/vercel.json` → `relatedProjects: ["<app-id>"]`.
- **Blocker for executor**: project IDs must be fetched via `vercel project ls` or Vercel dashboard before this phase. Plan will note this as a prerequisite.

**Phase 3 — Rename `project-urls.ts` → `related-projects.ts` and migrate to helper**
- 5 files renamed: `apps/app/src/lib/`, `apps/platform/src/lib/`, `apps/www/src/lib/`, `api/platform/src/lib/`, plus `apps/app/src/lib/microfrontends.ts` (which resolves `wwwUrl`) folded into the new `related-projects.ts`.
- Each file becomes ~3 lines (call `createRelatedProjectResolver`).
- Update all 6 import sites (2 tRPC routes, 2 docs layouts, `provider-configs.ts`, plus `next.config.ts` rewrite block).

**Phase 4 — Eliminate inline duplications**
- `apps/app/next.config.ts:21-30` — delete inline `platformUrl`, import from `./src/lib/related-projects` (note: this is already a noted CodeRabbit nitpick — Theme 6.3 in research).
- `api/platform/src/lib/oauth/authorize.ts:20-33` — replace inline `VERCEL_ENV`/`VERCEL_URL` derivation with the migrated helper.
- `api/platform/src/lib/oauth/callback.ts:23-36` — same.

- Current logic uses `env.NODE_ENV === "development"` to switch dev vs prod allowlist. In Vercel preview, `NODE_ENV === "production"` → preview deploys get the prod allowlist `["lightfast.ai", "*.lightfast.ai"]`, which doesn't match the actual preview origin.
